items.title: practice_facts table + posts.previous_content column exist and are reversible
steps: 1. psql against dev DB: \dt practice_facts
2. psql against dev DB: \d website_builder.posts (confirm previous_content jsonb column)
3. Read down() in 20260701000000_add_practice_facts_and_post_snapshot.ts, confirm it drops the column then the table
items.expected: Both objects exist; down() cleanly reverses both in dependency order
items.evidence: Direct psql query confirmed both objects present on dev DB; down() reviewed line-by-line in spec.html Rev 1 follow-up — not live-rolled-back since it shares a batch with an unrelated teardown migration (see Revision Log Rev 1)
items.notes: Live rollback intentionally skipped to avoid undoing an unrelated ledger reconciliation in the same batch; down() correctness verified by code inspection instead

items.title: PracticeFactModel enforces tenant scope and supports idempotent re-extraction
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/PracticeFactModel.test.ts
items.expected: create/createMany contract + failure path pass; findByOrgAndLocation never returns another org's rows; deleteByPageId/deleteByPostId support clear-and-replace
items.evidence: 14/14 tests pass, including 4 tenant-isolation tests proving org A never sees org B's rows

items.title: Fact extraction discards any fact whose source_excerpt isn't a verbatim substring of the input (structural no-fabrication enforcement)
items.precondition: Seeded dev page with known business_data/page_content
steps: 1. Run verifyFacts() against a mocked LLM response containing one sourced fact and one fabricated fact with a non-matching source_excerpt
2. Run PracticeFactModel.deleteByPageId -> createMany twice in sequence against a real seeded dev-DB page
items.expected: Sourced fact kept, fabricated fact discarded; row count does not grow on the second run (idempotent)
items.evidence: Mocked-response test: 1 fact kept, 1 fabricated fact discarded, all assertions passed. Idempotency: row count stayed at 1 after both runs against org 6 / a real seeded page, then cleaned up to 0
items.notes: Full live LLM call not exercised in this pass (no Anthropic key invocation) — the structural validation and idempotency, which are the actual fabrication backstop, were proven directly against real data instead

items.title: Generation consumes only sourced facts and auto-applies via recoverable write paths
items.precondition: Seeded page and post with practice_facts rows
steps: 1. npx vitest run src/__tests__/service.seo-generation.test.ts
2. Confirm log line '[SEO Generation] GEO opening_content_recommendation applied as new page version' appears, with no corresponding live-page-row update call
3. Confirm log line '[SEO Generation] GEO opening_content_recommendation applied; previous content snapshotted' appears for the post case
items.expected: Page auto-apply creates a new version row, never updates the live row in place; post auto-apply snapshots previous_content before overwriting; empty opening_content_recommendation triggers no write at all
items.evidence: 4/4 new tests pass; both log lines observed directly during the full-suite run (127/127 tests, 16/16 files) confirming the exact mechanism described in the spec's Constraints section

items.title: New fact-extraction/listing routes are correctly wired with auth middleware
steps: 1. curl -i http://localhost:3000/api/admin/websites/:id/pages/:pageId/seo/facts (no auth header)
2. curl -i -X POST same URL (no auth header)
3. Repeat for the /posts/:postId/seo/facts equivalents
items.expected: All 4 routes resolve (no 404) and return 401 Authentication required for unauthenticated requests
items.evidence: Live curl against the local dev server confirmed routing + auth middleware wiring for all 4 new routes

items.title: Authenticated round trip: trigger extraction, then list returns the extracted facts with provenance
items.precondition: Valid admin JWT, a real project with at least one page
steps: 1. POST /api/admin/websites/:id/pages/:pageId/seo/facts with a valid JWT
2. Poll/wait, then GET the same path
3. Confirm returned facts include fact_text and source_excerpt
items.expected: 200 { success: true, data: { facts: [...] } } with non-empty source_excerpt on each fact
items.evidence: Live authenticated round trip against org 46 (Tri-City Endodontics) project d5810549-.../page c483b9f5-...: POST returned {job_id}, GET after the job completed returned 9 real facts (ZEISS EXTARO 300 microscope, Morita Veraviewepocs 3D R100, Morita AdvErL EVO laser, etc.), each with a source_excerpt independently confirmed to be a verbatim quote from the page's actual HTML content. Test rows deleted from practice_facts after verification.
items.notes: Found and fixed two real bugs during this run: (1) extractPageFacts/extractPostFacts crashed with 500 on a client request with no body (destructuring req.body when undefined) instead of a clean 400 — fixed with `req.body || {}` in both handlers. (2) The extraction worker's fetchBusinessData gate short-circuited BEFORE checking page/post content — a page with zero business_data but rich, extractable page content (this exact test page) got zero facts purely because the org's business_data JSON was null. Fixed: content is checked first, business_data absence now logs and proceeds with page/post content only instead of skipping extraction entirely. Both fixes verified live after the change (400 returned correctly; 9 real facts extracted from the previously-null-business_data page).

items.title: SEO panel surfaces extracted facts, GEO recommendations, and auto-apply provenance in the browser
items.precondition: Authenticated admin session, a project with a page and a post that has been through SEO generation
steps: 1. Navigate to a page's editor, open the SEO panel, open the 'significant' section tab
2. Confirm the practice facts list renders with expandable source excerpts
3. Confirm GEO fields (target query, opening content recommendation, FAQ candidates) render when present
4. Confirm the auto-apply banner appears when a newer draft page version exists
5. Repeat steps 1-4 for a post via PostsEditorView, confirming the previous-vs-current content compare renders
items.expected: All elements render without console errors; source excerpts are legible and not buried; auto-apply banner correctly reflects version/snapshot state
items.evidence: Live browser session (preview tools, JWT-authenticated as a super admin) against /admin/websites/d5810549-.../pages/.../edit: opened the SEO panel's Structured Data tab, confirmed 'GEO / ANSWER-FIRST RECOMMENDATIONS' and 'VERIFIED PRACTICE FACTS' sections render with clear provenance copy ('only keeps facts it can quote back to source — nothing is invented'). Clicked the real 'Extract Facts' button end to end: job triggered, facts appeared automatically in the UI without a manual reload, expanding a fact card revealed 'SOURCE — PAGE CONTENT' with the literal quoted excerpt. Screenshots captured at each step.
items.notes: Found and fixed a real bug during this run: the original fixed-6-second-delay-then-single-refetch assumed extraction 'finishes in low single-digit seconds', but observed real LLM latency was ~20-30s — the single refetch fired before the job finished, leaving the UI stuck on 'No facts extracted yet' until a manual reload. Replaced with a bounded poll (~41s ceiling across 9 attempts) that stops as soon as facts appear. A second bug surfaced while verifying the fix: one transient mid-poll 500 aborted the whole poll loop silently (unhandled rejection in the mutation's onSuccess); fixed by wrapping each poll attempt in try/catch so a single failed attempt doesn't cancel the remaining schedule. Re-tested end to end after both fixes: facts appeared in the browser automatically, with zero manual reloads, confirming the fix. Test rows deleted from practice_facts after each verification pass; JWT scratch file removed afterward.

items.title: Bulk-generation live per-item progress: n/n counter opens a popover with pending/processing/done/failed groups, updating in real time (pages)
items.precondition: Authenticated admin session; a bulk SEO generation running on a project's pages list
steps: 1. Migration: confirm item_statuses jsonb column on seo_generation_jobs (T8)
2. Trigger a bulk SEO generate on a project's pages list
3. Confirm the worker seeds a full pending list, then flips one item to processing at a time, then done/failed (T9)
4. Confirm getBulkSeoStatus/getActiveBulkSeoJob return item_statuses (T10)
5. Click the n/n counter mid-run; confirm a popover opens grouping items Processing -> Pending -> Done -> Failed, updating live with no page reload (T11)
items.expected: Exactly one item processing at a time; popover groups correctly and updates live via the existing 2s poll; no page reload needed
items.evidence: Full live run in the browser against sandbox org 45 (is_sandbox=true), project acc7cbfc, 8-page batch, JWT-authenticated as super admin. DB polling during the run confirmed: full pending list seeded before any item started, exactly one item 'processing' at a time, all 8 'done' at completion. Live DOM inspection confirmed the n/n counter is now a clickable button that opens the grouped popover ('PROCESSING (1) /about, PENDING (6), DONE (1) /', progressing to 8/8) with live updates and no reload; outside-click closes it. Backend curl mid-run confirmed item_statuses in both getBulkSeoStatus and getActiveBulkSeoJob responses.
items.notes: T9's per-item update uses an atomic SQL-level jsonb update (not read-modify-write) per §10.5. T11 built a hand-rolled popover following the existing ColorPicker.tsx pattern (no shared Popover primitive exists in the codebase). preview_screenshot had a broken port-forward this session; evidence is live DOM state via preview_eval, not screenshot images. Sandbox test job rows deleted; sandbox page seo_data/version rows left in place (isolated sandbox data, deleting scattered version rows risks corrupting the sandbox project's version sequence for zero real-world benefit).

items.title: Bulk-generation live per-item progress popover reused on the posts side
items.precondition: Authenticated admin session; an active bulk SEO job for a post type
steps: 1. Confirm PostTypeSeoButton reuses T11's BulkSeoProgressPopover component without forking it
2. Confirm the posts n/n counter is a clickable trigger wired to the popover with items={status.item_statuses}
3. Confirm the posts bulk-status hook (useBulkSeoProgress) surfaces item_statuses from the getActiveBulkSeoJob endpoint it polls
items.expected: Posts n/n counter opens the same grouped live popover as pages; item_statuses flows through the exact endpoint the posts hook polls
items.evidence: Code verified: PostTypeSeoButton.tsx imports BulkSeoProgressPopover from the shared SeoPanel path (not forked, §4.3), converts the counter to a button toggling isProgressPopoverOpen, and renders the popover with items={status.item_statuses}; prop contract matches the component's interface exactly. Frontend tsc -p tsconfig.app.json passes with zero errors (proves status.item_statuses exists on the hook's type and the wiring is type-correct). Data path proven live: seeded a real active post job (entity_type=post, a real post_type_id, item_statuses with one item in each of processing/pending/done/failed) and hit the exact endpoint useBulkSeoProgress polls — GET /:id/seo/bulk-generate/active?entity_type=post&post_type_id=... returned all 4 item_statuses correctly. The popover component that renders this data is the same one live-proven in a real browser in T11 (pages side), fed the identical data shape.
items.notes: A full posts-side browser screenshot was NOT captured: the preview browser genuinely hangs in this session (reproduced directly — preview_eval timed out on navigation; this is the same failure that stalled two T12 agents at the 600s watchdog). No sandbox org has any posts/post-types, so a genuine posts bulk run would require building a synthetic template->post_type->posts->LLM-run fixture chain. Instead, T12's verification is: (a) identical reused component already live-proven in T11, (b) matching prop contract + passing frontend tsc, (c) the exact posts-hook API endpoint proven to return item_statuses via a real seeded job + authenticated curl. Seeded post_type + job row deleted afterward (confirmed 0 remaining). This is verification-by-equivalence for the render, plus direct proof of the data path — stated honestly rather than claiming a screenshot that the broken preview tooling could not produce.
items.waiver: Posts-side popover render not captured as a browser screenshot due to a session-wide preview-browser hang (reproduced firsthand). Mitigated by: identical component live-proven on the pages side (T11), matching prop contract with passing frontend tsc, and the exact posts-hook endpoint proven live to return item_statuses. Residual risk is limited to the ~15-line mount wiring, which type-checks and mirrors the live-verified pages wiring.
